calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 267131 The Estate of James Lawrence Ellington.

Appearances: Bruce Feder for Chandler Flickinger. Charles Wolff for San Francisco Opera Association. Kevin Solan for Great America Insurance Company.

Mr. Feder stated that he had a proposed order for the Kampen matter that was signed off by all Parties but that Mr. Wollf had an issue with the Ellington order. Mr. Wolff stated that the Kampen order adequately protects his Client's interest with respect to a possible appeal, that there was an issue with the Ellington order, that he had no problem with the text drafted by Mr. Feder, but that he was uncomfortable with the power of the Court with entering an amended order and therefore he did not sign the Ellington order. Both matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Continued To Feb-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 267131 the Estate of James Lawrence Ellington.

Appearances: Thomas Latham for San Francisco Opera House. Rowena Navia for Chandler Flickinger. Kevin Solan for Great American Assurity.

Mr. Latham requested a case management conference be set in the first week of February and that the December 18, 2009 evidentiary hearing date be vacated. In addition, he stated that discovery was needed and that he would like to attend mediation prior to February. Furthermore, he stated that a $100,000.00 preliminary distribution would be appropriate and that he would prepare a stipulation with regard to said distribution. Ms. Navia stated that she would present it to her Client and that she was not willing to stipulate to anything at this point. Mr. Solan stated that the responses to the interrogatory questions were due in December, that he needed to take depositions and that discovery would be completed by January. The Court will sign the order referring the matters to pro bono mediation. Mediation is to be completed by January 26, 2010. Counsel is to inform the Examiner as to the outcome of mediation. Both matters were continued to February 2, 2010. An evidentiary hearing will be set on that date if needed. The December 18, 2009 evidentiary hearing date was vacated.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Nov-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Nov-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 267131 Estate of James Lawrence Ellington.

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Chandler Flickinger. Kevin Solan for Great American Insurance Co. Thomas Latham for The San Francisco Opera Association.

Ms. Navia stated that she had been contacted on Friday, that she had spoken to opposing Counsel and requested a 30 day continuance. The Court suggested mediation. Ms. Navia stated that she thought her Client would not object to mediation. Mr. Latham stated that he did not object and Mr. Solan stated that mediation was fine as long as it was done after the accounting was filed. The Court stated that it was debatable as to how you construe the damages and that it was unclear as to what the judicial decision would be. The accounting on the Kampen matter was moved from October 13, 2009 to November 24, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A case management conference on both matters will also be held on November 24, 2009 and the Parties are to make a determination regarding mediation. The evidentiary hearing on calendar for October 23, 2009 for both matters was continued to December 18, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-21-2009 Continued To Sep-16-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 267131 Estate of James Lawrence Ellington.

Appearances: Kevin Solan for Assurity Great American Insurance Company. Charles Wolff for The San Francisco Opera Association (Petitioner). Chandler Flickinger appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Wolff stated that an informal accounting was presented to him, that the accounting appeared to be sufficiently in order, that there were a couple of discrete points/issues that should be entered in the surcharge petitions and that discovery needed to be conducted. Furthermore, Mr. Wolff stated that he would file papers with regard to what would be subject to the surcharges by August 11, 2009. An evidentiary hearing on the surcharge issues for both matters was set for October 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. With regard to the Kampen matter, Mr. Solan stated that the Inventory and Appraisal was filed this morning and that the accounting would be filed by July 31, 2009. Mr. Wolff stated that the Ellington matter was ready to be closed except for the surcharge issue. A status/case management conference on both matters was set for September 16, 2009 at 10:30 a.m. Mr. Flickinger will appear by telephone/court call. Counsel are to submit a joint case management brief by September 11, 2009 to the court. Mr. Wolff will prepare an order after hearing.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR#10276. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting

Petition To Surcharge And Remove Executor For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Continued To Jun-02-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chandler Flickenger appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. Charles Wolff for the San Francisco Opera Association. Arthur Park for Great America Insurance Company.

Mr. Flickinger reported that the assets were in three bank accounts, and stated that closing the estate would not be a problem but that there may be a tax issue with the sale of the framing business. The Court ordered Mr. Flickinger to search for copies of all of the bank statements from 1999 to current and to provide copies to Mr. Wolff and Mr. Park by April 29, 2009. In addition, the Court directed Mr. Flickinger to file the Petition For First and Final Account and For Final Distribution by May 6, 2009. The matter was continued to June 2, 2009 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a hearing on the Accounting Petition and all other issues. If it turns out that Mr. Flickinger's inactions end up costing the estate, surcharges may be considered.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
Continued From 07/10 To 07/24
